steps.title: Find the known total
steps.content:
The $9$ listed integers have mean $41$, so their total is:
$$9\cdot 41=369$$

steps.title: Limit the new mean
steps.content:
Let $x$ be the missing integer and $M$ be the mean of all $10$ integers. Since each integer is less than $60$, $x<60$. If $M\geq 43$, then:
$$x=10M-369\geq 430-369=61$$
That is too large, so the integer mean must be $42$.

steps.title: Find the missing integer
steps.content:
Use the mean of all $10$ integers:
$$\frac{369+x}{10}=42$$
$$369+x=420$$
$$x=420-369$$
The listed values are all less than the missing value. The answer is 51.
